perturbation.original_text: 1.12 "Product" shall mean TARA-002 and any similar products.
perturbation.explanation: This change narrows the definition of 'Product' to exclusively refer to TARA-002, excluding any 'similar products.' This contradicts the original broad definition and creates uncertainty in sections that discuss the application of the agreement to similar products, potentially limiting the scope of the license and royalty obligations to TARA-002 only. The impact would be especially felt in instances where 'similar products' might have been considered covered under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.4 "Field" means all therapeutic, diagnostic and prophylactic uses of the Product(s).
perturbation.justification: The modification narrows the definition of 'Product' from including 'any similar products' to 'TARA-002 only.' This creates a direct contradiction with section 1.4, which defines 'Field' as uses of 'the Product(s),' implying the agreement covers multiple products. By limiting 'Product' to only TARA-002, the modified definition contradicts the original intent and creates uncertainty regarding the scope of 'Field' concerning any similar products.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 License. University hereby grants to ArTara an exclusive license to use the Program Data solely for the Project and in Regulatory Filings in the Field in the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 License. University hereby grants to ArTara a non-exclusive license to use the Program Data solely for the Project and in Regulatory Filings in the Field in the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: The modification from an 'exclusive' to a 'non-exclusive' license introduces ambiguity. It now allows the University to potentially license the same Program Data to other parties. This contradicts the intent established in other sections that ArTara has proprietary rights. It directly impacts the value and security of ArTara's investment in the Project, causing uncertainty in other sections, because the exclusivity of the license is removed, creating the possibility that third parties can now utilize the Program Data.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE FOUR DATA AND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY - 4.2
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 Right of Reference and IND Assignment. University hereby grants to ArTara an exclusive Right of Reference to all Program Regulatory Filings by University in support of the Product. Upon written request of ArTara, University will assign the IND to ArTara.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants ArTara an "exclusive license," while the changed text grants a "non-exclusive license." This directly contradicts section 4.3, which grants ArTara an "exclusive Right of Reference" based on the assumption that ArTara holds exclusive rights to the Program Data. The change introduces ambiguity and undermines the exclusivity intended by the "exclusive Right of Reference," creating a significant contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8.2 Termination by Either Party. Either Party may terminate the Project and all commitments and obligations with respect thereto, subject to Section 8.3 herein, upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other Party. In the event of any termination of the Project by University, (a) University agrees to complete Phase I and II of the Project, and (b) ArTara will continue to provide annual funding until the completion of Phase II. Upon termination of the Project by ArTara this Agreement will terminate subject to Section 8.3 and ArTara will reassign to University the IND if assignment thereof previously occurred pursuant to Section 4.3.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.2 Termination by Either Party. Either Party may terminate the Project and all commitments and obligations with respect thereto, subject to Section 8.3 herein, upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other Party. In the event of any termination of the Project by University, (a) University agrees to use reasonable efforts to complete Phase I and II of the Project, and (b) ArTara will continue to provide annual funding until the completion of Phase II, contingent to University completion of phase 1. Upon termination of the Project by ArTara this Agreement will terminate subject to Section 8.3 and ArTara will reassign to University the IND if assignment thereof previously occurred pursuant to Section 4.3.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'agrees to complete' to 'agrees to use reasonable efforts to complete', a contradiction is introduced. It changes the strict obligation on the University to complete the stated phases to a weaker, effort-based commitment. This affects ArTara's assurance of receiving the deliverables expected from these phases and its certainty about continuing funding obligation, because the changed obligation creates an in-text contradiction by adding contingency on University completing phase 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Funding. During the term of the Project in accordance with the Project Plan, ArTara will provide thirty thousand dollars (US $30,000) per year in funding for the Project, taking into consideration the time spent by University employees required for the Project. The Parties agree to discuss in good faith potential additional funding required for completion of the Project as applicable and necessary.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated University to 'complete Phase I and II', while the changed text weakens this to 'use reasonable efforts to complete Phase I and II'. This directly contradicts the financial commitment outlined in Section 3.1, which states ArTara will provide funding 'during the term of the Project in accordance with the Project Plan'. The change introduces ambiguity regarding University's obligation and therefore ArTara's funding commitment becomes uncertain.
